A pair of travelers pass you by on the road. An Elf and a Dwarf, who you overhear talking about their last visit to Grand Central.
An elderly man in wizard robes comes trudging past you down the road in the opposite direction. By the sigil on their cloak you can tell they're a member of the Arcturian Society.
You hear a carriage come trundling down the road behind you. Looking back you see a tinker's caravan pulled by two horses.
You pass by a group of travelers headed in the opposite direction, they're all talking amongst themselves and laughing at occasional jokes.
You've got the road all to yourself here, there's nobody else in sight.
A person stands lost and confused at the edge of the road. They ask you which way to the Four Bridges?
The road ahead is uncobbled and particularly muddy, you hear your boots squelching in the damp dirt with each step.
Someone dropped a few coins on the ground here. Finders keepers.

Someone dropped a few coins on the ground here. Finders keepers.

Success: You spot a pickpocket moving through the crowd towards you as you lean over to pick them up.
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't pick up these coins, they're nailed to the ground. Your coinpurse feels lighter. You lost 1d4 gp.

You spot something glinting just off the edge of the road!

Success: You find a shiny new dagger.
Failure: Despite your efforts you don't find anything.

You spot something glinting just off the edge of the road!

Success: You find a discarded arrow.
Failure: Despite your efforts you don't find anything.

You spot something glinting just off the edge of the road!

Success: You find a shiny new shield.
Failure: Despite your efforts you don't find anything.

You spot something glinting just off the edge of the road!

Success: You find a perfectly useable cookpot.
Failure: Despite your efforts you don't find anything.

You spot something glinting just off the edge of the road!

 It's just rubbish.

You spot some scattered grey runes abandoned at the side of the road

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A weathered shrine stands a short distance from the edge of the road. You decide to stop for a quick rest. Looking up at the shrine you wonder for which deity was it made? A few purple runes materialise in your hands.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Misty Step within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Invisibility within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of See Invisibility within.
Failure: It's empty.

You come across a discarded scroll case.

Success: You find a Scroll of Alarm within.
Failure: It's empty.
A wooden wagon creaks slowly down the road in the opposite direction.
The road here is dry and dusty, kicking up small clouds with each step.
A crooked signpost leans beside the road, its markings long faded.
A pair of travelers pass quietly without saying a word.
Several flat stones line the edge of the road like old markers.
Birdsong drifts from somewhere off the roadside.
A heavily loaded cart crawls slowly along the road ahead.
You pass the cold ashes of an old roadside campfire.
Fresh wagon tracks run along the road ahead of you.
A lone merchant walks beside a mule carrying bundles of goods.
A simple wooden bench sits beside the road for weary travelers.
The sky overhead is filled with drifting clouds.
A strong wind rushes along the open road.
An abandoned wagon wheel lies discarded beside the road.
You hear travelers ahead singing a cheerful road song.
A tired horse stands tied beside the road while its owner rests nearby.
A rider on horseback trots past without slowing.
A weathered stone marker stands beside the road.
A narrow side path branches off from the main road.
Leaves scatter across the road as the wind picks up.
A cloud of dust drifts slowly along the road from distant travelers.
Wildflowers grow along the edge of the road.
A shepherd leads a few sheep slowly along the road.
You spot a clearing beside the road often used by travelers to rest.
The road becomes uneven with loose stones underfoot.
You hear the faint jingling of harness bells approaching.
A bard strolls down the road strumming a quiet tune.
A passing cloud casts a long shadow over the road.
Several feathers drift slowly across the path.
Tall trees cast long shadows across the road.
Deep ruts mark the well traveled road.
A small group of pilgrims walks slowly along the road.
A distant rider appears briefly on the horizon.
The road becomes covered in loose gravel.
A courier rides past quickly, clearly in a hurry.
The road here is worn smooth from years of travel.
An empty handcart sits abandoned by the roadside.
You see a caravan slowly moving along the road far ahead.
The warm sun shines down on the open road.
A cool breeze blows steadily along the path.
Two traders walk past arguing quietly about prices.
A Revenue Guild broadsheet blew into the ditch—stall fees, grace weeks, and whose ledgers never quite balance. If you want the dry version in full, try `!library tax`.
Carved notches on a post match a traveler's cramped notes: safe camps, toll bends, where floods wash the cobbles bare. It points you toward longer road atlases—`!library travel`.
A roadside shrine's box holds a mildewed leaflet on shrines, rival sects, and soup offered without hidden oaths. Pilgrims say the longer copy helps—`!library religion`.
A broken wooden sign lies half buried beside the road.
Small spirals of dust spin across the road.
A lone tree stands beside the road offering shade.
A small caravan of wagons creaks slowly past.
Several cloaked travelers pass by quietly.
A large rock sits beside the road, worn smooth by time.
You pause for a moment to take in the quiet road.
Fresh horse tracks mark the dirt road.
Dust drifts lazily across the road.
Two hunters walk down the road carrying bows.
A wagon rolls slowly along the road far ahead.
For a while the road grows quiet and peaceful.
Wind blown branches lie scattered across the road.
A lantern hangs from a roadside post.
A farmer walks beside a small cart of supplies.
Old tracks slowly fade into the dirt road.
A rusty horseshoe lies in the dust.
The road stretches far into the distance.
A pair of travelers walks past quietly discussing their journey.
A sudden gust of wind sweeps across the road.
You take a moment to rest beside the road.
You hear the distant creaking of wagon wheels.
Clouds drift slowly across the sky above the road.
A silent caravan of pack animals walks past.
A thin line of smoke rises somewhere beyond the road.
A messenger rushes down the road on horseback.
A quiet monk walks steadily along the road.
A broken wooden crate lies beside the road.
Small stones roll down the road as the wind blows.
A simple wooden post marks the edge of the road.
Several wagons roll slowly down the road together.
A coil of rope lies forgotten beside the road.
Your footsteps echo quietly along the empty road.
A pair of traders leads their pack animals along the road.
You pause briefly in the shade beside the road.
You hear distant laughter from travelers somewhere ahead.
The road bends slightly as the wind whistles past.
Your boots grow dusty from the long road.
The road here widens slightly, showing signs of frequent caravan travel.
A torn banner flutters weakly from a roadside pole, its symbol long faded.
You see scraps of wood and iron where someone repaired a wagon wheel.
A traveler walks beside you for a time in silence before eventually parting ways.
Your cloak gathers a fine layer of dust from the constant road travel.
You hear faint bells, likely from pack animals somewhere ahead.
A small pile of stones marks something forgotten beside the road.
The wind rushes across nearby grasslands, whispering alongside the road.
Faint, older tracks cross the road at odd angles.
A cloaked traveler glances at you briefly before continuing on.
A small roadside well sits dry and abandoned.
A snapped leather harness lies discarded in the dust.
A flattened patch of grass suggests many travelers rest here.
A single bird watches from a post as you pass.
You catch the lingering scent of a recently extinguished fire.
Several wagon tracks split off toward a lesser path.
You hear raised voices far down the road, though you can't make out words.
A wrapped bundle lies by the roadside, long since emptied.
A small stone statue stands overlooking the road.
A pair of armed guards march past with purpose.
A small coin purse lies half buried in the dirt.
Old toll coins lie scattered near a broken post.

A dim blue rune flickers briefly near the roadside.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.

A small stone glows faintly with runic energy.

A few old Westmarch runes shimmer here, left as a narrative reward for servers that track them.
A grateful traveler tosses you a few coins before leaving.

You find tools scattered near the road.

Success: You salvage a usable kit.
Failure: Nothing is usable.

A length of rope lies abandoned.

Success: You recover it.
Failure: It falls apart.

Herbs grow along the roadside.

Success: You gather some.
Failure: They crumble.

You find broken arrows.

Success: You salvage arrowheads.
Failure: They are useless.

Cloth scraps lie nearby.

Success: You collect usable cloth.
Failure: They're too worn.
You come across the remnants of a recently departed caravan site. The ground is churned, and faint voices echo in the distance. Something about the hurried departure feels unusual.
As dusk falls, faint lights drift along the road ahead before vanishing entirely when approached.
Your footsteps echo strangely here, as if the road itself carries sound farther than it should.
A simple grave sits beside the road, marked only by a stone. Fresh flowers rest atop it.
You reach an old crossroads with no signs. The air feels heavier here, as though many choices were made at this exact spot.
A robed scholar mutters notes while walking.
The remains of a shrine crumble beside the road.
A soft voice sings somewhere unseen.
A crate lies open and empty.
A nearby horse snorts as you pass.
A warped track suggests a damaged wagon passed through.
You take a moment to breathe and continue.
A cloaked figure stands briefly before moving on.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Flametongue** Enchanted over 30 days, requires a base weapon to enchant, using 3 Rubies worth at least 100gp each along with 3 CR 5 Runic Shards. You must cast a spell that produces Fire Damage to complete this enchant.
Failure: It's beyond you.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Band of Loyalty** Requires a base ring to enchant, over the course of 2 days, use gem dust worth at least 100 gp along with a CR 1 Runic Shard and the spell Bestow Curse.
Failure: It's beyond you.

You come across something interesting...

Success: You learn something new! **Charlatan's Die** Requires the base Dice Set to enchant along with a casting of Prestigitation and a CR 1 Runic Shard. Enchant over the course of 1 day.
Failure: It's beyond you.

You come across a nugget of amber!

Success: You pry it out of some rocks.
Failure: Despite your efforts you spoil the amber as you break it free.

You come across some herbs at the edge of the road!

Success: You pick a few garlic cloves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some herbs at the edge of the road!

Success: You pick a few mint leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herb.

You come across some herbs at the edge of the road!

Success: You pick a few arnica leaves!
Failure: Despite your efforts you can't harvest the herbs.
The breeze rushes past you carrying the noise of travellers passing by from the road.
You feel you'd have more luck fishing at a river.
You feel you'd have more luck fishing at the sea.
It feels like ages since you caught anything.
Another person comes down to the water to fish a short distance from you.

Your line goes taught, you've got a fish on the hook!

Success: You make short work if and catch a carp!
Failure: Despite your efforts the line snaps and the fish gets away.
